requirements.title: Queue Initialization
requirements.description: The queue module shall initialize from a caller-provided backing array.
requirements.rationale: FIFO queues are essential for message passing, event buffering, and inter-module communication in embedded systems; initializing from a caller-provided array satisfies the zero-allocation constraint.

requirements.title: Queue Initial State
requirements.description: The queue shall set start index and length to zero on initialization.
requirements.rationale: Zeroing the start index and length on initialization establishes a known-empty state, ensuring the queue is immediately usable without undefined behavior from uninitialized fields.

requirements.title: Queue Enqueue
requirements.description: The queue shall copy an element to the back of the circular buffer in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: O(1) enqueue via a circular buffer provides deterministic timing for real-time message producers.

requirements.title: Queue Full Rejection
requirements.description: The queue shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_SIZE_ERROR when enqueue is attempted on a full queue.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on a full queue surfaces the overflow condition to the caller rather than silently dropping or overwriting data.

requirements.title: Queue Dequeue
requirements.description: The queue shall remove and copy out the front element in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: O(1) dequeue via a circular buffer provides deterministic timing for real-time message consumers.

requirements.title: Queue Empty Dequeue Rejection
requirements.description: The queue shall return JUNO_STATUS_ERR when dequeue is attempted on an empty queue.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on an empty queue surfaces the underflow condition rather than returning stale or uninitialized data.

requirements.title: Queue Peek
requirements.description: The queue shall return a pointer descriptor to the front element without removing it in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: Peek allows consumers to inspect the next element without consuming it, which is useful for conditional dequeue logic and priority-based processing.

requirements.title: Queue Empty Peek Rejection
requirements.description: The queue shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_SIZE_ERROR when peek is attempted on an empty queue.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on empty peek prevents the caller from receiving a descriptor to uninitialized or stale memory.
